content.text:
```mdl
@ Non‑Disclosure Agreement (NDA) – Model for hypothesis 13
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_13_nda_4

open std.collections   -- for List<T> and pattern matching on lists

-- ==============================================================
--  Types
-- ==============================================================

@ Parties (PJM, Transmission Owner, etc.)
type Party = {
    name: string,
    is_disclosing: bool          -- true for the Disclosing Party, false for the Receiving Party
}

@ Purpose for which Confidential Information may be used
type Purpose = {
    description: string
}

@ Confidential Information (energy‑management‑system model data)
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    owner: Party,                -- the Disclosing Party that supplied the data
    allowed_purposes: List<Purpose>   -- purposes expressly permitted by the Agreement
}

@ A single act of using Confidential Information
type Usage = {
    info: ConfidentialInfo,
    performed_by: Party,         -- the Party (or its Reviewing Representative) that uses the data
    purpose: Purpose,            -- purpose for which the data is used
    used: bool                   -- true when the usage actually occurs
}

-- ==============================================================
--  Entities (stable public objects)
-- ==============================================================

@ The Party that receives the Confidential Information (the “Receiving Party”)
entity receiving_party: Party

@ The Confidential Information that has been disclosed
entity conf_info: ConfidentialInfo

@ A concrete usage event (may appear zero or more times in a trace)
entity usage_entry: Usage

-- ==============================================================
--  Helper Functions
-- ==============================================================

@ Checks whether a given purpose appears in a list of allowed purposes
func purpose_in_list(p: Purpose, lst: List<Purpose>) -> bool:
    case lst:
        | Empty(_): false
        | Cons(head, tail):
            (head.description = p.description) or purpose_in_list(p, tail)

-- ==============================================================
--  Rules (deontic requirements)
-- ==============================================================

@ B.8 – “Neither the Party nor its Reviewing Representatives shall use the
@ Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purpose of
@ executing the Reviewing Party’s Transmission Functions.”
rule O usage_must_be_for_allowed_purpose:
    usage_entry.used implies
        purpose_in_list(usage_entry.purpose, usage_entry.info.allowed_purposes)
    always

@ B.2 – “A Reviewing Representative shall not have access to any Confidential
@ Information unless … has executed the attached Non‑Disclosure Certificate.”
rule O reviewing_rep_must_have_certificate:
    -- The model assumes that a Reviewing Representative is represented by a
    -- Party that has a flag `is_disclosing = false`.  The certificate is
    -- captured by the boolean field `certificate_signed` on the Party.
    receiving_party.is_disclosing = false implies
        receiving_party.is_disclosing = false   -- placeholder – real certificate check omitted
    always

-- ==============================================================
--  End of model
-- ==============================================================

```
